title: Hyalolaena transcaspica
intro: Hyalolaena transcaspica, also known as Carpesium transcaspicum, is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, commonly found in Central Asia and the Caucasus region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in treating respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Hyalolaena transcaspica include carpesin, a sesquiterpene lactone known for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat coughs, colds, and skin irritations, and to promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Hyalolaena transcaspica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It belongs to the genus Hyalolaena and is native to the Caspian region. The plant is often categorized as a member of the tribe Scandiceae within the family Apiaceae. Further classification places it under the subfamily Apioideae. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and genetic characteristics.
